rawHead:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- breadcrumb-head-auto-begin -->

rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 Quaternions 2 Rotations 3 Cayley–Dickson 4 Octonions 5 Frobenius 6 Hurwitz

Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras

Hamilton carved $i^2=j^2=k^2=ijk=-1$ on a Dublin bridge in 1843. Doubling that algebra one more time gives the octonions; doubling again breaks. Frobenius and Hurwitz tell us why the tower stops at $\mathbb{O}$ — and why $1,2,4,8$ are the only dimensions where multiplication can preserve length.

blocks.html:
1. Quaternions $\mathbb{H}$

The quaternions $\mathbb{H}=\mathbb{R}\langle 1,i,j,k\rangle$ form a 4-dimensional real algebra defined by the single bracelet of relations Hamilton carved on Brougham Bridge:

$$i^2 = j^2 = k^2 = ijk = -1.$$

From these one derives $ij=k$, $jk=i$, $ki=j$ — and reversing any factor flips the sign: $ji=-k$, $kj=-i$, $ik=-j$. Multiplication is associative (one realisation: $\mathbb{H}\hookrightarrow M_2(\mathbb{C})$, sending $1,i,j,k$ to specific Pauli-like matrices) but emphatically not commutative. Every element $q=a+bi+cj+dk$ has a conjugate $\overline q=a-bi-cj-dk$ and norm $$|q|^2 = q\,\overline q = a^2+b^2+c^2+d^2,$$ giving $\mathbb{H}$ the underlying inner-product space $\mathbb{R}^4$. For $q\neq 0$ the inverse is $q^{-1}=\overline q/|q|^2$, so $\mathbb{H}$ is a (skew) division algebra.

Quaternion multiplication tester
enter two quaternions $p,q$ and inspect $pq$, $qp$, the commutator, $|pq|$ vs $|p||q|$.
click multiply to compute.

Format: a+bi+cj+dk with integer or decimal coefficients (signs and zero terms optional).
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const root = document.getElementById('quat-mult');
  if(!root) return;
  function parseQ(s){
    s = s.replace(/\s+/g,'').replace(/-/g,'+-');
    if(s.startsWith('+')) s = s.slice(1);
    const out = [0,0,0,0];
    const keys = {'':0,'i':1,'j':2,'k':3};
    s.split('+').filter(Boolean).forEach(t => {
      const m = t.match(/^(-?\d*\.?\d*)([ijk]?)$/);
      if(!m) return;
      let coef = m[1];
      if(coef === '' || coef === '+') coef = 1;
      else if(coef === '-') coef = -1;
      else coef = parseFloat(coef);
      out[keys[m[2]]] += coef;
    });
    return out;
  }
  function mul(p,q){
    const [a,b,c,d] = p, [e,f,g,h] = q;
    return [
      a*e - b*f - c*g - d*h,
      a*f + b*e + c*h - d*g,
      a*g - b*h + c*e + d*f,
      a*h + b*g - c*f + d*e
    ];
  }
  function norm2(q){ return q.reduce((s,x)=>s+x*x,0); }
  function fmt(q){
    const labels = ['','i','j','k'];
    const parts = [];
    q.forEach((c,k)=>{
      if(Math.abs(c) < 1e-9) return;
      const sign = c < 0 ? '-' : (parts.length ? '+' : '');
      const mag = Math.abs(Math.abs(c) - 1) < 1e-9 && k > 0 ? '' : Math.abs(c).toFixed(2).replace(/\.?0+$/,'');
      parts.push(sign + mag + labels[k]);
    });
    return parts.join('') || '0';
  }
  function go(){
    const p = parseQ(document.getElementById('qm-p').value);
    const q = parseQ(document.getElementById('qm-q').value);
    const pq = mul(p,q), qp = mul(q,p);
    const comm = pq.map((x,i)=>x-qp[i]);
    const np = Math.sqrt(norm2(p)), nq = Math.sqrt(norm2(q)), npq = Math.sqrt(norm2(pq));
    document.getElementById('qm-out').innerHTML =
      'pq = ' + fmt(pq) + '\n' +
      'qp = ' + fmt(qp) + '\n' +
      '[p,q] = pq − qp = ' + fmt(comm) + '\n' +
      '|p| = ' + np.toFixed(3) + ',  |q| = ' + nq.toFixed(3) + ',  |pq| = ' + npq.toFixed(3) + '\n' +
      '|p||q| = ' + (np*nq).toFixed(3) + '   ✓ multiplicativity holds';
  }
  document.getElementById('qm-go').addEventListener('click', go);
  document.getElementById('qm-rand').addEventListener('click', ()=>{
    const r = ()=> (Math.random()*4-2).toFixed(1);
    document.getElementById('qm-p').value = r()+'+'+r()+'i+'+r()+'j+'+r()+'k';
    document.getElementById('qm-q').value = r()+'+'+r()+'i+'+r()+'j+'+r()+'k';
    go();
  });
  go();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: The imaginary quaternions $\mathrm{Im}\,\mathbb{H}=\{bi+cj+dk\}$ form a 3-dimensional subspace, naturally identified with $\mathbb{R}^3$. Restricted to imaginaries, the bracket $[u,v]=uv-vu$ is twice the cross product $2(u\times v)$, and $u\overline v + v\overline u = -2\langle u,v\rangle$ recovers the dot product. The quaternions therefore package both products of $\mathbb{R}^3$ into a single associative law.

See also
  • Field extensions and minimal polynomials
  • Groups, rings, and fields

Used in
Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras · Frobenius's theorem
Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras · The Cayley–Dickson tower
Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras · Unit quaternions and $\mathrm{SU}(2)\to\mathrm{SO}(3)$
</section>

2. Unit quaternions and the cover $\mathrm{SU}(2)\to\mathrm{SO}(3)$

The unit quaternions $\mathrm{Sp}(1)=\{q\in\mathbb{H}:|q|=1\}$ are the 3-sphere $S^3\subset\mathbb{R}^4$, equipped with quaternion multiplication. They form a compact Lie group, isomorphic as a Lie group to $\mathrm{SU}(2)$ via $a+bi+cj+dk\mapsto\bigl(\begin{smallmatrix}a+bi&c+di\\-c+di&a-bi\end{smallmatrix}\bigr)$. The Lie algebra is $\mathrm{Im}\,\mathbb{H}\cong\mathfrak{su}(2)\cong\mathfrak{so}(3)$ — three dimensions of pure imaginary, with bracket = quaternion commutator.

Each unit $q$ acts on $\mathrm{Im}\,\mathbb{H}\cong\mathbb{R}^3$ by conjugation: $$\rho_q\colon v\longmapsto q\,v\,q^{-1}=q\,v\,\overline q.$$ The map $q\mapsto\rho_q$ is a smooth group homomorphism $\mathrm{Sp}(1)\to\mathrm{SO}(3)$. If $q=\cos(\theta/2)+\sin(\theta/2)\,\hat n$ for a unit imaginary $\hat n$, then $\rho_q$ is the rotation through angle $\theta$ about $\hat n$. The half-angle is the source of the famous double cover: $q$ and $-q$ implement the same rotation, so $\ker\rho=\{\pm 1\}$ and $\mathrm{SO}(3)\cong\mathrm{Sp}(1)/\{\pm 1\}\cong\mathbb{RP}^3$.

Quaternion rotation visualiser
drag the angle slider; the cube rotates by $\rho_q$ about the chosen axis.
60°
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('rot-svg');
  if(!svg) return;
  const cx = 260, cy = 140, sc = 60;
  // cube vertices
  const verts = [];
  for(let x=-1;x<=1;x+=2) for(let y=-1;y<=1;y+=2) for(let z=-1;z<=1;z+=2) verts.push([x,y,z]);
  const edges = [];
  for(let i=0;i<8;i++) for(let j=i+1;j<8;j++){
    let d = 0; for(let k=0;k<3;k++) if(verts[i][k]!==verts[j][k]) d++;
    if(d===1) edges.push([i,j]);
  }
  function project(p){
    // simple isometric-ish projection
    const [x,y,z] = p;
    return [cx + sc*(x*0.95 - z*0.55), cy + sc*(-y + z*0.30)];
  }
  function rotate(q, v){
    // v = [0, vx, vy, vz] ; conjugation q v q^-1
    const [a,b,c,d] = q;
    // q * v
    const t0 =       - b*v[0] - c*v[1] - d*v[2];
    const t1 =   a*v[0]       + c*v[2] - d*v[1];
    const t2 =   a*v[1] - b*v[2]       + d*v[0];
    const t3 =   a*v[2] + b*v[1] - c*v[0]      ;
    // (q*v) * conj(q) = (q*v) * (a,-b,-c,-d)
    const r1 =  t0*(-b) + t1*a   + t2*(-d) - t3*(-c);
    const r2 =  t0*(-c) - t1*(-d) + t2*a   + t3*(-b);
    const r3 =  t0*(-d) + t1*(-c) - t2*(-b) + t3*a;
    return [r1,r2,r3];
  }
  function axisVec(){
    const k = document.getElementById('rot-axis').value;
    if(k==='x') return [1,0,0];
    if(k==='y') return [0,1,0];
    if(k==='z') return [0,0,1];
    const s = 1/Math.sqrt(3);
    return [s,s,s];
  }
  function redraw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // axes
    const axes = [['#e07a5f','x',[1.6,0,0]],['#83c167','y',[0,1.6,0]],['#58c4dd','z',[0,0,1.6]]];
    axes.forEach(([col,lbl,p])=>{
      const [u,v] = project(p);
      const [u0,v0] = project([0,0,0]);
      const ln = SVG('line',{x1:u0,y1:v0,x2:u,y2:v,stroke:col,'stroke-width':1,opacity:0.55});
      svg.appendChild(ln);
      const t = SVG('text',{x:u+6,y:v+4,'font-size':11,fill:col}); t.textContent=lbl; svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // axis of rotation
    const ax = axisVec();
    const [au,av] = project([ax[0]*1.7, ax[1]*1.7, ax[2]*1.7]);
    const [au0,av0] = project([-ax[0]*1.7,-ax[1]*1.7,-ax[2]*1.7]);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:au0,y1:av0,x2:au,y2:av,stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.5,'stroke-dasharray':'4,3',opacity:0.7}));
    // build q from theta
    const theta = +document.getElementById('rot-theta').value * Math.PI/180;
    const half = theta/2;
    const q = [Math.cos(half), Math.sin(half)*ax[0], Math.sin(half)*ax[1], Math.sin(half)*ax[2]];
    // rotate verts
    const R = verts.map(v => rotate(q,v));
    const P = R.map(project);
    // edges
    edges.forEach(([i,j])=>{
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:P[i][0],y1:P[i][1],x2:P[j][0],y2:P[j][1],stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.6,opacity:0.85}));
    });
    // verts
    P.forEach(([u,v])=> svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:u,cy:v,r:2.5,fill:'var(--violet)'})));
    // mark a tagged vertex
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:P[7][0],cy:P[7][1],r:5,fill:'var(--yellow)',stroke:'#111','stroke-width':1.2}));
    // readout
    const fmt = x => (x>=0?'+':'') + x.toFixed(3);
    document.getElementById('rot-out').textContent =
      'q = '+q[0].toFixed(3)+fmt(q[1])+'i'+fmt(q[2])+'j'+fmt(q[3])+'k\n' +
      '|q| = '+Math.sqrt(q.reduce((s,x)=>s+x*x,0)).toFixed(4)+
      '   (rotation angle = '+(+document.getElementById('rot-theta').value)+'° about ('+ax.map(x=>x.toFixed(2)).join(', ')+'))\n' +
      'note q and −q give the same rotation — the SU(2) → SO(3) double cover.';
    document.getElementById('rot-theta-val').textContent = document.getElementById('rot-theta').value+'°';
  }
  document.getElementById('rot-axis').addEventListener('change', redraw);
  document.getElementById('rot-theta').addEventListener('input', redraw);
  redraw();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: Half-angles, full rotations: setting $\theta=2\pi$ in the slider sends $q\to-1$, but the cube is back where it started. You need $\theta=4\pi$ for $q$ itself to return to $1$ — the spinor's signature.

See also
  • Axioms and matrix examples
</section>

3. The Cayley–Dickson tower

Given a real $*$-algebra $A$ (associative, with conjugation $a\mapsto a^*$), the Cayley–Dickson construction produces a new algebra $A'=A\oplus A$ with

$$(a,b)(c,d) = (ac - d^*b,\ da + bc^*),\qquad (a,b)^* = (a^*,-b).$$

Iterating from $A_0=\mathbb{R}$ (where $a^*=a$) generates the canonical chain

$$\mathbb{R}\subset\mathbb{C}\subset\mathbb{H}\subset\mathbb{O}\subset\mathbb{S}\subset\cdots,$$

each step doubling dimension. At every doubling something is sacrificed, and the loss is sharp: once a property fails, no later algebra recovers it.

Cayley–Dickson ladder
click a rung to inspect the algebra and what it lost.
click a rung above.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('cd-svg');
  if(!svg) return;
  const rungs = [
    {x:60,  name:'ℝ', dim:1,  props:['real','self-conj','comm','assoc','alt','div','norm-mult'], lost:''},
    {x:170, name:'ℂ', dim:2,  props:['—','—','comm','assoc','alt','div','norm-mult'], lost:'self-conjugation x* = x'},
    {x:290, name:'ℍ', dim:4,  props:['—','—','—','assoc','alt','div','norm-mult'], lost:'commutativity (ij ≠ ji)'},
    {x:410, name:'𝕆', dim:8,  props:['—','—','—','—','alt','div','norm-mult'], lost:'associativity'},
    {x:530, name:'𝕊', dim:16, props:['—','—','—','—','—','—','—'],          lost:'alternativity, division (zero divisors!), norm-mult'}
  ];
  // backbone line
  svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:30,y1:120,x2:570,y2:120,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1}));
  rungs.forEach((r,i)=>{
    const fill = i<=3 ? 'var(--cyan)' : 'var(--pink)';
    const c = SVG('circle',{cx:r.x,cy:120,r:24,fill:'var(--panel2)',stroke:fill,'stroke-width':2.4,style:'cursor:pointer'});
    c.dataset.idx = i;
    svg.appendChild(c);
    const t = SVG('text',{x:r.x,y:126,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':17,fill:'#fff','font-weight':600,style:'cursor:pointer'});
    t.textContent = r.name; t.dataset.idx = i;
    svg.appendChild(t);
    const dim = SVG('text',{x:r.x,y:165,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    dim.textContent = 'dim '+r.dim;
    svg.appendChild(dim);
    if(i>0){
      const prev = rungs[i-1];
      drawArrow(svg, [prev.x+24, 120], [r.x-24, 120], {color:'var(--mute)',markerId:'cd-a',width:1.2});
      const lt = SVG('text',{x:(prev.x+r.x)/2, y:104,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--pink)','font-style':'italic'});
      lt.textContent = '×2'; svg.appendChild(lt);
    }
  });
  // section title
  const head = SVG('text',{x:300,y:30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
  head.textContent = 'each doubling adds dimension and loses one structure';
  svg.appendChild(head);
  function pick(i){
    const r = rungs[i];
    const checks = ['self-conj','comm','assoc','alt','div','norm-mult'];
    const labels = {
      'self-conj':'x* = x',
      'comm':'commutative',
      'assoc':'associative',
      'alt':'alternative (x(xy) = (xx)y)',
      'div':'division algebra',
      'norm-mult':'|xy| = |x||y|'
    };
    const lines = ['Algebra: '+r.name+'   (dim_ℝ = '+r.dim+')'];
    if(r.lost) lines.push('Newly lost: '+r.lost);
    lines.push('');
    checks.forEach(p=>{
      const idx = r.props.indexOf(p);
      const has = idx >= 0;
      lines.push((has?'  ✓ ':'  ✗ ') + labels[p]);
    });
    document.getElementById('cd-out').textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', ev => {
    const i = ev.target && ev.target.dataset && ev.target.dataset.idx;
    if(i !== undefined) pick(+i);
  });
  pick(2);
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
algebra$\dim_{\mathbb R}$commutativeassociativealternativedivision
$\mathbb{R}$$1$✓✓✓✓
$\mathbb{C}$$2$✓✓✓✓
$\mathbb{H}$$4$—✓✓✓
$\mathbb{O}$$8$——✓✓
$\mathbb{S}$ (sedenions)$16$————

Used in
Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras · Octonions $\mathbb{O}$
</section>

4. Octonions $\mathbb{O}$

The octonions $\mathbb{O}$ are the Cayley–Dickson double of $\mathbb{H}$. As a real vector space they are $\mathbb{R}^8$ with basis $\{1,e_1,\dots,e_7\}$, and the multiplication is conveniently encoded by the Fano plane: a triangle with three medians and an inscribed circle, whose seven oriented lines list the seven triples that multiply like $\{i,j,k\}$.

If a directed line passes through $e_a\to e_b\to e_c$, then

$$e_a e_b = e_c,\quad e_b e_c = e_a,\quad e_c e_a = e_b,\qquad e_b e_a = -e_c,\quad\text{etc.}$$

Each of the 7 oriented lines therefore generates a copy of the quaternions $\mathbb{H}\hookrightarrow\mathbb{O}$. But triples that don't lie on a common line associate badly: $e_1(e_2 e_4) \neq (e_1 e_2)e_4$ in general. What survives is the alternative law: $$x(xy)=(xx)y,\qquad (yx)x = y(xx).$$ Equivalently (Artin's theorem), every subalgebra of $\mathbb{O}$ generated by two elements is associative.

Fano plane multiplication oracle
click two basis vectors; the oracle reads the product off the unique line through them.
click two distinct $e_i$.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('fano-svg');
  if(!svg) return;
  // place 7 points: 3 corners, 3 midpoints, 1 centre
  const w = 320, h = 280, ox = 20, oy = 25;
  const A = [ox + w/2, oy], B = [ox, oy+h], C = [ox+w, oy+h];
  const mAB = [(A[0]+B[0])/2, (A[1]+B[1])/2];
  const mBC = [(B[0]+C[0])/2, (B[1]+C[1])/2];
  const mCA = [(C[0]+A[0])/2, (C[1]+A[1])/2];
  const O = [(A[0]+B[0]+C[0])/3, (A[1]+B[1]+C[1])/3];
  // labels: choose a standard convention
  // lines (index triples), each oriented x→y→z meaning xy=z, yz=x, zx=y
  // we use: e1 = A, e2 = B, e3 = C, e4 = mAB, e5 = mBC, e6 = mCA, e7 = O
  const pts = { e1:A, e2:B, e3:C, e4:mAB, e5:mBC, e6:mCA, e7:O };
  const lines = [
    ['e1','e2','e4'], // side AB through midpt mAB
    ['e2','e3','e5'],
    ['e3','e1','e6'],
    ['e1','e5','e7'], // median A → mBC through centre
    ['e2','e6','e7'],
    ['e3','e4','e7'],
    ['e4','e5','e6']  // inscribed circle
  ];
  // draw triangle sides
  function drawLine(p, q, opts={}){
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:p[0],y1:p[1],x2:q[0],y2:q[1],stroke:opts.stroke||'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':opts.w||1.5,opacity:opts.o||0.6}));
  }
  drawLine(A,B); drawLine(B,C); drawLine(C,A);
  drawLine(A, mBC,{stroke:'var(--violet)',w:1.4,o:0.55});
  drawLine(B, mCA,{stroke:'var(--violet)',w:1.4,o:0.55});
  drawLine(C, mAB,{stroke:'var(--violet)',w:1.4,o:0.55});
  // inscribed circle (the line e4-e5-e6)
  // approximate: centre at O, radius = distance from O to mAB
  const rad = Math.hypot(mAB[0]-O[0], mAB[1]-O[1]);
  svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:O[0],cy:O[1],r:rad,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.4,opacity:0.55}));
  // points
  const labelOff = { e1:[0,-12], e2:[-14,4], e3:[14,4], e4:[-14,-2], e5:[14,-2], e6:[0,-12], e7:[0,-14] };
  Object.entries(pts).forEach(([k,p])=>{
    const c = SVG('circle',{cx:p[0],cy:p[1],r:8,fill:'var(--panel2)',stroke:'var(--blue)','stroke-width':1.6,style:'cursor:pointer'});
    c.dataset.id = k;
    svg.appendChild(c);
    const off = labelOff[k] || [10,4];
    const t = SVG('text',{x:p[0]+off[0],y:p[1]+off[1],'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--ink)','font-weight':600,style:'pointer-events:none'});
    t.textContent = k.replace('e','e');
    svg.appendChild(t);
  });
  // selection state
  let sel = [];
  function lookup(a,b){
    if(a===b) return {prod:'-1', line:'(e_i² = -1)'};
    for(const [x,y,z] of lines){
      const set = [x,y,z];
      if(set.includes(a) && set.includes(b)){
        // determine orientation
        const seq = [x,y,z,x,y,z];
        for(let i=0;i<3;i++){
          if(seq[i]===a && seq[i+1]===b) return {prod:seq[i+2], line:`${x}→${y}→${z}`, sign:'+'};
        }
        // reverse
        for(let i=0;i<3;i++){
          if(seq[i]===b && seq[i+1]===a) return {prod:seq[i+2], line:`${x}→${y}→${z}`, sign:'-'};
        }
      }
    }
    return null;
  }
  function update(){
    const $x = document.getElementById('fano-x'), $y = document.getElementById('fano-y');
    $x.textContent = sel[0] || '—';
    $y.textContent = sel[1] || '—';
    if(sel.length<2){ document.getElementById('fano-out').textContent='click two distinct e_i.'; return; }
    const r = lookup(sel[0], sel[1]);
    if(!r){ document.getElementById('fano-out').textContent='not on a common Fano line.'; return; }
    const sign = r.sign === '-' ? '−' : '+';
    document.getElementById('fano-out').textContent =
      sel[0]+' · '+sel[1]+' = '+sign+r.prod+'\n'+
      'line: '+r.line+'\n'+
      'reverse: '+sel[1]+' · '+sel[0]+' = '+(r.sign==='-'?'+':'−')+r.prod+'   (anti-commute)';
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', ev=>{
    const id = ev.target && ev.target.dataset && ev.target.dataset.id;
    if(!id) return;
    if(sel.length>=2) sel = [];
    sel.push(id);
    update();
  });
  document.getElementById('fano-clear').addEventListener('click', ()=>{ sel = []; update(); });
})();
</script>

blocks.html: Twelve facts encoded in seven lines. Listing the multiplication table by hand requires $7\cdot 6=42$ entries; the Fano plane compresses them to seven oriented triples, each generating a quaternion subalgebra.

Used in
Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras · Hurwitz's theorem
</section>

5. Frobenius's theorem

Theorem (Frobenius, 1877). The only finite-dimensional associative division algebras over $\mathbb{R}$ are $\mathbb{R}$, $\mathbb{C}$, and $\mathbb{H}$.

Sketch. Let $D$ be such an algebra. Pick $x\in D\setminus\mathbb{R}$. Its minimal polynomial is real and irreducible, hence linear or an irreducible quadratic — so $\mathbb{R}[x]\cong\mathbb{R}$ or $\mathbb{C}$. If $D=\mathbb{R}[x]$ we are done. Otherwise the orthogonal complement $V=\{x:\tr(x)=0,\,x^2\le 0\}$ to $\mathbb{R}\cdot 1$ is a real Euclidean space (use the trace as inner product). For $u,v\in V$ unit vectors, $u^2=-1$ and $(uv+vu)\in\mathbb{R}$. Anti-commuting unit pairs $u,v$ generate a subalgebra $\cong\mathbb{C}$ extended by $v$, i.e. $\cong\mathbb{H}$. A third unit vector $w$ anti-commuting with $u,v$ forces $w=\pm uv$ by associativity — there is no room for a fourth. So $\dim V\in\{0,1,3\}$ and $\dim D\in\{1,2,4\}$.

The case-tree of Frobenius
click a leaf to see why no fourth unit imaginary fits.
click a node above.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('frob-svg');
  if(!svg) return;
  const root = [300, 40];
  const n2  = [300, 110];
  const n3a = [120, 200], n3b = [300, 200], n3c = [480, 200];
  const n4a = [200, 280], n4b = [400, 280];
  const explain = {
    R: 'D = ℝ.   No element outside ℝ — done.',
    C: 'D = ℝ[x] for some x ∉ ℝ.   Then x² ∈ ℝ_{<0}, so ℝ[x] ≅ ℂ.',
    Hcase: 'Pick u ∈ V with u² = -1, then a second unit v ∈ V ⊥ u with v² = -1 and uv = -vu.   The subalgebra ⟨1,u,v,uv⟩ has dimension 4 and the quaternion relations: D ⊇ ℍ.',
    Hexact: 'D = ⟨1,u,v,uv⟩ ≅ ℍ — and we are done.',
    nofourth: 'A third unit imaginary w ⊥ {u,v}, anti-commuting with both.   Then uw and vw are both unit imaginaries anti-commuting with one of u,v.   Associativity forces w = ±uv, contradicting w ⊥ uv.   So no fourth dimension fits.'
  };
  const arr = (p,q)=> drawArrow(svg, p, q, {color:'var(--mute)', markerId:'frob-a', width:1.2});
  arr(root, n2);
  arr(n2, n3a); arr(n2, n3b); arr(n2, n3c);
  arr(n3c, n4a); arr(n3c, n4b);
  function node(p, label, key, fill){
    const r = SVG('rect',{x:p[0]-70, y:p[1]-18, width:140, height:36, rx:8, ry:8,
      fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:fill||'var(--blue)','stroke-width':1.4, style:'cursor:pointer'});
    r.dataset.k = key;
    svg.appendChild(r);
    const t = SVG('text',{x:p[0],y:p[1]+5,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'#fff','font-weight':600,style:'pointer-events:none'});
    t.textContent = label;
    svg.appendChild(t);
  }
  node(root, 'Pick x ∈ D \\ ℝ', 'top');
  node(n2, 'm.p.(x) deg ≤ 2', 'mp');
  node(n3a, 'D = ℝ', 'R', 'var(--cyan)');
  node(n3b, 'D = ℝ[x] ≅ ℂ', 'C', 'var(--cyan)');
  node(n3c, 'D ⊋ ℝ[x]', 'Hcase', 'var(--yellow)');
  node(n4a, 'D = ℍ', 'Hexact', 'var(--yellow)');
  node(n4b, 'no 4th unit', 'nofourth', 'var(--pink)');
  svg.addEventListener('click', ev=>{
    const k = ev.target && ev.target.dataset && ev.target.dataset.k;
    if(k && explain[k]) document.getElementById('frob-out').textContent = explain[k];
  });
  document.getElementById('frob-out').textContent = explain.Hcase;
})();
</script>

blocks.html: Drop associativity and the result fails: the octonions are an 8-dimensional real division algebra. Drop "division" and the result fails: $\mathbb{R}\oplus\mathbb{R}$ is a 2-dimensional commutative associative real algebra with zero divisors. Frobenius's theorem balances on exactly the conditions stated.

See also
  • Field extensions and minimal polynomials

Used in
Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras · Hurwitz's theorem
</section>

6. Hurwitz's theorem

A composition algebra over $\mathbb{R}$ is a (not-necessarily-associative) unital algebra equipped with a non-degenerate quadratic form $N$ satisfying the multiplicative law

$$N(xy) = N(x)\,N(y)\qquad\text{for all }x,y.$$

Theorem (Hurwitz, 1898). The only finite-dimensional real composition algebras are $\mathbb{R}$, $\mathbb{C}$, $\mathbb{H}$, and $\mathbb{O}$, of dimensions $1$, $2$, $4$, and $8$.

The dimensions $1,2,4,8$ are exactly the ones at which Cayley–Dickson still produces a division algebra. They also coincide with the dimensions $n$ for which $\mathbb{R}^n$ admits a continuous bilinear product without zero divisors — a deep theorem of Bott–Milnor and Kervaire (independently, 1958), proved using the cohomology of real projective spaces.

The norm-multiplicativity in $\mathbb{H}$ is the famous four-square identity of Euler:

$$\bigl(a^2+b^2+c^2+d^2\bigr)\bigl(e^2+f^2+g^2+h^2\bigr) = (ae-bf-cg-dh)^2+(af+be+ch-dg)^2+(ag-bh+ce+df)^2+(ah+bg-cf+de)^2.$$

The eight-square identity follows similarly from $\mathbb{O}$. A nine-square identity does not exist; this is one of the most striking elementary consequences of Hurwitz's theorem.

Where the tower stands
click an algebra; the bar shows which classification each lives in.
click an algebra above.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('hurwitz-svg');
  if(!svg) return;
  const xs = [70, 180, 290, 400, 510];
  const algs = ['ℝ','ℂ','ℍ','𝕆','𝕊'];
  const dims = [1,2,4,8,16];
  const bands = [
    {y: 60, label:'Frobenius (assoc. division algebras over ℝ)', col:'var(--cyan)',  ok:[true,true,true,false,false]},
    {y:110, label:'Hurwitz (composition algebras over ℝ)',       col:'var(--yellow)',ok:[true,true,true,true,false]},
    {y:160, label:'division algebra (allow non-assoc)',          col:'var(--green)', ok:[true,true,true,true,false]},
    {y:210, label:'Cayley–Dickson tower',                        col:'var(--violet)',ok:[true,true,true,true,true]}
  ];
  // header
  algs.forEach((a,i)=>{
    const r = SVG('rect',{x:xs[i]-32, y:18, width:64, height:30, rx:7, ry:7,
      fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--blue)','stroke-width':1.4, style:'cursor:pointer'});
    r.dataset.idx = i;
    svg.appendChild(r);
    const t = SVG('text',{x:xs[i],y:38,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,fill:'#fff','font-weight':600,style:'pointer-events:none'});
    t.textContent = a; svg.appendChild(t);
  });
  // bands
  bands.forEach(b=>{
    // band text
    const lt = SVG('text',{x:20,y:b.y+5,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lt.textContent = b.label;
    svg.appendChild(lt);
    // dots
    b.ok.forEach((ok,i)=>{
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:xs[i], cy:b.y, r:9,
        fill: ok? b.col : 'transparent',
        stroke: ok? b.col : 'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.6}));
    });
  });
  function pick(i){
    const lines = [
      'algebra: '+algs[i]+'   (dim_ℝ = '+dims[i]+')',
      ''
    ];
    bands.forEach(b => lines.push((b.ok[i]?'  ✓ ':'  ✗ ') + b.label));
    if(i===4) lines.push('\n𝕊 has zero divisors: (e_1+e_{10})(e_5-e_{14}) = 0 in one basis.');
    if(i===3) lines.push('\n𝕆 is the largest composition algebra — Hurwitz stops here.');
    if(i===2) lines.push('\nℍ is the largest associative one — Frobenius stops here.');
    document.getElementById('hurwitz-out').textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', ev=>{
    const i = ev.target && ev.target.dataset && ev.target.dataset.idx;
    if(i!==undefined) pick(+i);
  });
  pick(3);
})();
</script>

blocks.html: The four classifications nest: $\{\mathbb R,\mathbb C,\mathbb H\}\subset\{\mathbb R,\mathbb C,\mathbb H,\mathbb O\}\subset\text{division algebras}\subset\text{Cayley–Dickson tower}$. Each step is a strict inclusion, and each strict inclusion costs a structural property.
</section>

Connections

The 1, 2, 4, 8 phenomenon reaches well beyond algebra. The same dimensions parametrise the parallelisable spheres ($S^0,S^1,S^3,S^7$), the Hopf fibrations $S^1\to S^3\to S^2$ and $S^3\to S^7\to S^4$ and $S^7\to S^{15}\to S^8$, and the exceptional Lie group $G_2=\operatorname{Aut}(\mathbb O)$. Open frontiers include the role of $\mathbb O$ in $11$-dimensional supergravity, the $E_8$ lattice realised inside $\mathbb O\otimes\mathbb O$, and the non-commutative geometry of quaternionic and octonionic projective spaces.

  • Lie algebras — axioms — $\mathrm{Im}\,\mathbb{H}$ with quaternion commutator $[u,v]=uv-vu$ is exactly $\mathfrak{so}(3)\cong\mathfrak{su}(2)$.
  • Lie groups — SO(3) vs SU(2) — the unit quaternions are the Lie group $\mathrm{Sp}(1)\cong\mathrm{SU}(2)$, double-covering $\mathrm{SO}(3)$.
  • Representation theory — $\mathbb{H}$ is the unique non-commutative irreducible real division algebra, and underlies the type-H representations in real Schur theory.
  • Abstract algebra — field extensions — Frobenius's theorem refines the algebraic-closure fact "$[\mathbb C:\mathbb R]=2$" to a classification of associative division algebras.
  • Galois cohomology & Brauer — over $\mathbb{R}$ the Brauer group is $\mathrm{Br}(\mathbb R)=\mathbb Z/2$, with non-trivial class represented by $\mathbb H$ — Frobenius read cohomologically.
